req: REQ-026 (Platform Task Management (Linear as system of record))

issues.detail: The attempted negative only proves that a deliverable GitHub Issue does not become NGO-visible task state. It can still be created and managed privately as a GitHub Issue, contrary to “GitHub Issues (code bugs only)” and the coverage map’s claim that deliverables are NEVER managed there. This is independent of the queued category-wording drift.
issues.fix: Assert that no deliverable or scope task is created or retained as a GitHub Issue and that its authoritative task exists only in Linear.

issues.detail: The added fixture treats the volunteer, their agent, and the platform integration as three distinct actor identities whose provenance must be preserved. REQ-026 says agents act under the volunteer’s identity and their actions attribute to the volunteer; this also conflicts with AT-026.19.
issues.fix: For agent-originated events, assert accountable actor attribution to the volunteer. If implementation records an optional source-kind such as agent automation, distinguish it from Linear actor identity and do not require it as requirement-owned behavior.

issues.detail: No test covers “Coordinator review is a pilot spot-check.” AT-026.09 evaluates briefs against a rubric but never requires a coordinator spot-check or an observable review outcome.
issues.fix: Add a P0 test that exercises the coordinator’s pilot spot-check against a sample of the pushed decomposition and observes its recorded pass/fail outcome.

issues.detail: Both tests assert that an issue with an unresolved declared blocker is not offered as next-unblocked. The second assertion in AT-026.09 duplicates the dedicated pull-flow test AT-026.11.
issues.fix: Keep session-size/rubric and encoded dependency ordering in AT-026.09; leave next-task pullability exclusively to AT-026.11.

issues.detail: “When its PR completes” does not define whether the PR was created, closed, or merged, making the no-auto-merge assertion ambiguous. “The loop degrades” also names no observable degraded behavior, so silently ignoring the auth failure could pass.
issues.fix: Use a defined PR-created/ready event and assert it remains unmerged without authorized merge action. For auth failure, assert an observable degraded state such as an auth warning, continued session operation, and failed or queued task-system updates.

issues.detail: “Norm violations surface to the coordinator” invents monitoring/escalation behavior not stated by REQ-026. The requirement defines these as working norms; only the N-day inactivity case explicitly raises a coordinator flag.
issues.fix: Remove the invented coordinator-surfacing assertion. Test delivery of the norms and conformance of the specified default flows only.

issues.detail: The parenthetical “a status change not backed by a linked merged PR never survives” applies to every status, contradicting authorized platform cancellations in AT-026.18 and completion-time cancellation in AT-026.20. The test’s preceding requirement-faithful claim concerns only the path to Done.
issues.fix: Narrow the parenthetical to “a Done transition not backed by a linked merged PR never survives,” leaving authorized lifecycle cancellations outside that invariant.

issues.detail: The test proves that a volunteer can create a linked scope-addition task but never proves the exclusive clause “v1 scope-addition tasks are volunteer-created.” A platform-created scope-addition task could still pass the authority matrix.
issues.fix: Add a negative platform fixture showing that platform issue creation is limited to decomposition/lifecycle-owned cases and cannot originate a volunteer-accepted scope-addition task.

issues.detail: Ownership is probed only on a completed project and specifically for the Linear workspace. This can pass if repo/Lovable ownership transfers only at completion or if the gateway transfers, despite REQ-026 saying delivery infrastructure is NGO-owned and coordination infrastructure—Linear and the gateway—is platform-owned and never transfers. REQ-005.5 and REQ-012 say the NGO owns the app and repo throughout.
issues.fix: Probe ownership during in-progress and completed states, explicitly asserting repo/Lovable NGO ownership throughout and Linear/gateway platform ownership with no transfer.

issues.detail: Under a completed free-workspace fixture, “any paid tier would be platform-paid” is a counterfactual rather than an observable billing assertion.
issues.fix: Add a paid-tier billing sentinel or inspect the configured billing owner and assert that the payer account is platform-owned.

issues.detail: The coverage-map row for “current work, hierarchy, recent activity” cites only AT-010.06. That test covers hierarchy, statuses, and current work, not recent activity; AT-010.09 covers activity phrasing but does not establish recency. The explicit REQ-026 acceptance criterion therefore lacks honest mapped coverage for recent activity.
issues.fix: Add or cite a P0 test with timestamped activity fixtures proving that recent task activity is conveyed, then update the coverage row accordingly.
